paper_title: The fishing effort and stock biomass of trawl fishery in the South -East offshore waters of Vietnam

entities.description: The primary author of the research paper.

entities.id: South Research Sub-Institute for Marine Fisheries
entities.description: The institution located in Vietnam where the author is affiliated.
The main research field focusing on the assessment and management of fish stocks and fishing efforts.
The amount of fishing activity, often measured by vessel power or time spent fishing.
The total mass of a specific fish stock in a given area.
A method of fishing that involves pulling a fishing net through the water behind one or more boats.
The largest average catch that can be captured from a stock under existing environmental conditions.
A management tool used to visually assess fishing effort and stock biomass actuality compared to sustainable thresholds.
Software based on R programming language used for two-sided analysis of resource-fishing.
A programming language and free software environment for statistical computing and graphics.
Spreadsheet software used for data processing before running the Kobe Plot analysis.
Catch per unit effort calculation used as a proxy for stock biomass.

entities.id: Project: Research on fluctuations and distributions of fishing effort in the South-East offshore waters of Vietnam
entities.description: The primary numerical dataset used for analyzing trawler activities from 2008-2012.
The level by which fishing effort of trawl fishery exceeded the allowable yield.
The specific value by which the fishing effort of 150-249 hp trawlers exceeded the sustainable level in 2012.
The specific value by which the fishing effort of under 89 hp trawlers exceeded the sustainable level in 2012.
The specific timeframe during which the data was analyzed.
